mapping.a_element: m-element random subset S of [d] drawn from the external-field measure on the hypersimplex
mapping.b_element: a fully-packed singlet-loop/dimer covering of the corner-sharing lattice

mapping.a_element: covariance matrix Σ of the subset indicator vector X, whose pseudoinverse gives effective resistance
mapping.b_element: spin correlations within a fixed loop covering, determined by loop connectivity

brief_justification: The only overlap is the generic setting of combinatorial configurations on a graph/lattice plus the word 'correlation'; the actual machinery is disjoint. Paper A proves a spectral inequality bounding the effective resistance (e_i-e_j)^T Σ^† (e_i-e_j) of a strongly-Rayleigh subset measure's covariance to obtain a correlated-sampling stretch guarantee, whereas Paper B constructs a frustration-free AKLT-like spin-1 parent Hamiltonian from positive-semidefinite projectors whose zero-energy manifold is a loop state — no shared governing equation or process supports a role-for-role mapping.
